description_long: This object consists of a group of five terracotta vases and bowls, each with a distinct classical form. The collection includes two tall amphora-style vases, one with dual handles and another with a slender neck and no handles. There are also two round-bodied pots with wide mouths and prominent handles, as well as a shallow, open bowl with a thick rim. All pieces share a uniform matte, earthy brown color typical of unglazed clay pottery. The surfaces are smooth with subtle variations in curvature, and the handles are robust and integrated into the vessel bodies. The overall design is simple and functional, with no visible decorative motifs or embellishments.
description: A set of five matte terracotta vases and bowls with classical, handle-equipped forms and smooth, unadorned surfaces. The vessels vary in height and shape, all sharing an earthy brown color.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all five vessels, highlighting the variety in shapes and the presence of handles on three of them.
description_view_1: Angled view from the left, emphasizing the depth and roundness of the vessels and the thickness of the rims.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the two tallest vases, with their elongated necks and smooth, continuous surfaces.
description_view_3: Angled view from the right, displaying the open mouths of the vessels and the arrangement of the handles.
